title: Review round 1: Phase 1 Protocol Library (jmap-chat crate)
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — public credential fields, silent auth-header drops, and an undocumented call-id invariant will generate user-facing 401 issues and contributor mistakes; the unvalidated Id/UTCDate aliases and duplicate type definitions are API surface that will fossilize wrong before 1.0
Confidence: 80% chance of no missed bugs among the P0/P1/P2 issues. Uncertainty driven by: (1) code was not run or compiled during review — type-checking issues like 4u4.39 (Chat invalid field combinations) and 4u4.3/4u4.27 (auth encoding failures) were inferred structurally, not confirmed by test failures; (2) the SSE implementation was analyzed by code reading only, no live server to confirm the frame-too-large and last_event_id behaviors; (3) methods.rs was not seen — 4u4.33 (empty filter spec violation) and 4u4.42 (CHAT_USING allocation) were filed from architecture inference. Would raise confidence if: cargo test run against a live or mock JMAP server confirmed the P0 auth encoding and SSE bugs; fixture-based serialization tests verified Invocation wire format. Running out of real complaints: YES — 4u4.42 was the only P3 I labeled OPINION, and all remaining issues have an engineering basis I can articulate. The P3/P4 bucket is dominated by 'defend existing design decisions' issues (4u4.8, 4u4.11, 4u4.14, 4u4.20, 4u4.23, 4u4.26, 4u4.29, 4u4.35, 4u4.38, 4u4.40) rather than new complaints, which is a sign the review is near exhaustion.
close_reason: All P0/P1/P2 findings fixed; P3/P4 resolved or deferred; committed 238b818
